content: TYF should support arrival from existing material, including chat exports, loose source files, folders, or zip bundles, by preserving raw input under sources/imports, creating an orientation packet, minting source fragments when appropriate, and never writing imported material directly to manuscript. {subject=tyf.import.existing-material, value=preserve-orient-no-manuscript, surface=Codex session 2026-06-22 cold-start portability design}

TYF should define a portable author-work bundle format made of plain Markdown, YAML, and JSONL: canonical authored/workspace state remains inspectable in text files, git is optional, SQLite is derived/disposable, and a bundle marker or workspace-state marker lets agents recognize and re-enter an existing TYF workspace. {subject=tyf.portable.bundle-format, value=text-first-git-optional, surface=Codex session 2026-06-22 cold-start portability design}

TYF cold-start entrypoints should not require a title: an author or agent can start an untitled work, receive a stable generated work id, record title_status as unknown or working, and continue into source questions without blocking setup. {subject=tyf.cold-start.title, value=optional-title-nonblocking, surface=Codex session 2026-06-22 cold-start portability design}

TYF should treat unknown title, writing language, register, genre, form, and target structure as explicit open questions rather than setup blockers, recording them as unresolved author-facing questions and allowing safe source capture and orientation to continue. {subject=tyf.intake.unknowns, value=nonblocking-open-questions, surface=Codex session 2026-06-22 cold-start portability design}

TYF should let an author adopt direct manuscript edits as authoritative revisions: when a manuscript unit changes outside TYF, the author or agent can explicitly adopt the current file as the new base with evidence, preserve the previous version, record the author-originated revision, and allow future proposals against that base without tampering with write logs. {subject=tyf.manuscript.adopt-author-edit, value=author-edits-first-class, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF source fragments should be workspace-owned and reusable across works: a fragment may record its originating session or first-used work, but proposals for books, talks, newsletters, courses, essays, or later works can reference the same preserved fragment without duplicate capture. {subject=tyf.sources.workspace-owned, value=cross-work-fragment-reuse, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF first-session answers should be preserved as interview/source evidence rather than candidate drafts: onboarding packets should write raw author material under sources/interviews or sources/notes, mint source fragments where appropriate, and keep drafts reserved for candidate prose. {subject=tyf.first-session.source-evidence, value=intake-not-draft, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF should provide two author-facing front doors: a material-ingestion path that preserves and clusters existing notes before deciding works, and an elicitation path that interviews the author without requiring a settled title, form, or work type. {subject=tyf.frontdoors.material-and-elicitation, value=preserve-first-or-interview-first, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF should implement one complete semantic vertical slice from author statement to preserved source fragment, structured claim or example, exact supporting evidence, candidate passage, proposal shown to the author, recorded decision, and controlled manuscript write. {subject=tyf.vertical-slice.source-to-manuscript, value=author-evidence-to-controlled-write, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF should maintain meaningful longitudinal state and resume guidance: after significant acts it records active work and unit, current question, last author decision, unresolved tensions, pending proposals, and next useful move, and exposes them through a plain-language resume command. {subject=tyf.longitudinal.resume, value=meaningful-return-state, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF audits should become inspectable editorial records rather than boolean declarations: a passing audit requires an attached report with checked dimensions, findings or explicit no-finding statements, limitations, and dispositions for blocking issues. {subject=tyf.audit.editorial-record, value=findings-and-dispositions, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF author decisions should be grounded in a humane review contract: before acceptance the agent presents the candidate or diff, source and claim support, what changed, uncertainties, and available choices, while helper-level hashes, seals, locks, and paths remain implementation details. {subject=tyf.gate.author-review-contract, value=intelligible-approval-before-record, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF notices should distinguish fresh intake prompts from overdue gaps: immediately generated onboarding placeholders are staged as prompts or open questions, not nagging findings, and notices resurface only when age, stage, or relevance makes them useful. {subject=tyf.notice.stage-aware-intake, value=prompts-not-nags, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF documentation honesty should catch current public-command and version drift, including stale references to retired commands such as tyf write --confirm, mismatched version claims, pressure-test status contradictions, and event-log authority descriptions. {subject=tyf.docs.semantic-command-drift, value=manifesto-readme-validation-aligned, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF release packaging should exclude workshop residue and ship a clean author-facing object: skills, helper runtime, context, manifests, schemas, essential docs, and install validation should be included while .fbs history, caches, proof debris, duplicate packaging, and local construction artefacts stay out of releases. {subject=tyf.release.clean-object, value=author-facing-package, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF durability mechanisms should be proportionate to local-first authorship: keep atomic writes, previous-version backup, base detection, proposal/acceptance distinction, write history, and optional git snapshots, while reassessing heavyweight custody mechanisms unless they prevent a plausible authorship failure. {subject=tyf.durability.proportionality, value=local-first-amanuensis-not-custody, surface=Corrected TYF roast 2026-06-22}

TYF zip and folder arrivals should be containment-first: preserve the raw bundle under sources/imports, list its contents, detect TYF-shaped archives when possible, create an orientation/analysis packet with organization questions, and avoid unpacking or merging into live workspace surfaces until the author accepts a plan. {subject=tyf.import.bundle-containment, value=preserve-list-orient-before-merge, surface=Codex session 2026-06-22 arrival zip containment clarification}

content: TYF public writing runway should be the single canonical beta start path: authors and generated contexts begin with tyf start or tyf start <path>, title and structure stay optional, and no tyf today alias or backward-compatibility command remains in the public helper surface. {subject=tyf.public-path.start-canonical, value=start-public-no-today-alias, surface=Naming correction 2026-06-22}

TYF start should preserve first-session author answers as visible source evidence: tyf start and tyf start <path> update any provided working title or writing language in work.yaml, create or link a sources/interviews first-session packet, preserve optional arrivals through orientation, and point the writing runway to that packet before candidate prose begins. {subject=tyf.start.metadata-and-first-session-evidence, value=title-language-evidence-runway, surface=Naming correction 2026-06-22}

TYF beta should use a single-folder single-work model: a launched book repository is itself the work, with drafts, manuscript, outline, style-sheet, review records, and the writing runway at the workspace root; public docs and generated contexts must not make first-time authors reason about multiple works or an accidental today command. {subject=tyf.beta.single-folder-single-work, value=root-is-book-work-start-runway, surface=Naming correction 2026-06-22}

TYF should ship a proper Codex skill surface for book repositories: Codex installs target the current CODEX_HOME/skills or ~/.codex/skills location, the dispatcher skill has Codex UI metadata, generated/root AGENTS context routes new-book work through tyf start without waiting for a title, and onboarding docs explain that a book repo uses the installed skill plus its local AGENTS.md context. {subject=tyf.codex-skill.book-repo, value=codex-skill-install-and-start-context, surface=Naming correction 2026-06-22}
